message.content:
{"analysis":"I can see the image has two distinct regions:
- Top (rows 0-850): Blue sky with smooth gradient (B=255 always, R and G varying)
- Bottom (rows ~850+): Grayscale pattern with values 67 and 152, plus some reddish pixels around column 1050-1350

The bottom looks like a checkerboard or grid pattern. I need to understand the transition zone and the exact pattern structure better. Let me sample more extensively.","plan":"1. Examine the transition zone more carefully
2. Map out the bottom half pattern structure
3. Understand the top half wave pattern mathematically","commands":[{"keystrokes":"awk 'NR>3{y=int((NR-4)/2400); x=(NR-4)%2400; if(y>=830 && y<=870 && x%50==0){printf "(%d,%d): %s\n",y,x,$0}}' /app/image.ppm | head -60
","duration":15.0},{"keystrokes":"awk 'NR>3{y=int((NR-4)/2400); x=(NR-4)%2400; if(y==1000 && x%30==0){printf "(%d,%d): %s\n",y,x,$0}}' /app/image.ppm | head -80
","duration":15.0}]}
